steps.title: Apply the cube volume formula
steps.content: The volume of a cube equals the cube of its edge length. With edge 41 inches, we compute $V = 41^3$.

steps.title: Calculate 41 squared
steps.content:
First, compute $41 \times 41$:

$$41 \times 41 = 1,681$$

Note that 1,681 is choice B — a common error for students who use area instead of volume.

steps.title: Multiply by 41 once more
steps.content:
Now we compute $1,681 \times 41$ by splitting into tens and ones:

$$1,681 \times 40 = 67,240$$
$$1,681 \times 1 = 1,681$$
$$67,240 + 1,681 = 68,921$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
